title: [noob-test] 📚 Foundry Docs Noob Test Report - 2026-03-05
body:
### Summary Pages visited as a first-time user exploring Microsoft Foundry documentation: 1. **Home / Overview** — `overview/what-is-foundry` — Clear high-level pitch, but immediately encounters two portal versions ("new" vs. "classic") with no clear guidance on which to use. 2. **Quickstart: Set up Foundry Resources** — `get-started/quickstart-create-foundry-resources` — Well-structured with tab-based CLI/portal examples, but ends with a broken "Next step" card. 3. **Agent Development Overview** — `agents/development/overview` — Good content and code samples but has Windows-style backslash paths in links and broken `../../foundry-classic/...` references. 4. **SDK / API Reference** — `api-sdk/sdk-overview` — Excellent comparison table for picking an SDK, but overwhelming for a new user with 4 version tables (one per language), each with 1.x vs 2.x rows. 5. **Setup & Planning** — `setup/planning` — Comprehensive enterprise rollout guide, but too advanced as a starting point; lacks a clear pointer to a simpler first-time setup path. **Overall impression**: The docs are professionally written and well-organized for experienced Azure developers. For a complete beginner, the two-portal split, the naming history, and the number of SDK versions create unnecessary cognitive load right at the start. --- ### 🔴 Critical Issues Found #### 1. Broken "Next Step" card at end of Quickstart (blocking the happy path) **File**: `get-started/quickstart-create-foundry-resources.mdx`, line 266 The card after completing the main quickstart links to a non-existent path: ```mdx (Card title="Microsoft Foundry quickstart" icon="arrow-right" href="../quickstarts/get-started-code.md" /) ``` There is no `quickstarts/` directory. The correct file is `get-started/get-started-code.mdx`. This should be an absolute path: ```mdx (Card title="Microsoft Foundry quickstart" icon="arrow-right" href="/get-started/get-started-code" /) ``` #### 2. Broken "Next Step" card in get-started-code.mdx **File**: `get-started/get-started-code.mdx`, line 719 After completing the SDK quickstart, the user is directed to: ```mdx (Card title="Idea to prototype - Build and evaluate an enterprise agent" icon="arrow-right" href="../tutorials/developer-journey-idea-to-prototype.md" /) ``` There is no `tutorials/` directory. The correct file is `get-started/developer-journey-idea-to-prototype.mdx`. Should be: ```mdx (Card title="Idea to prototype - Build and evaluate an enterprise agent" icon="arrow-right" href="/get-started/developer-journey-idea-to-prototype" /) ``` #### 3. Windows-style backslash paths in agent overview (rendering broken on web) **File**: `agents/development/overview.mdx`, lines 148, 157, 183 Three links use Windows backslash separators which do not render as valid URLs: ```mdx [virtual network](how-to\virtual-networks.md) ``` These will likely render as broken or non-functional links. Should use forward slashes. #### 4. Empty href link in what-is-foundry.mdx **File**: `overview/what-is-foundry.mdx`, line 66 ````mdx [Switch to Microsoft Foundry (classic) documentation]() ``` This is a broken link with an empty `href`. A first-time user clicking this gets nowhere. It needs a real destination URL. #### 5. 299 broken relative links to `../../foundry-classic/...` Throughout `docs-vnext/`, there are **299 relative links** pointing to paths like `../../foundry-classic/agents/...`, `../../foundry-classic/how-to/...`, etc. These paths do not exist within the `docs-vnext/` tree and will 404 on the deployed site. Affected files include: - `agents/development/overview.mdx` — prerequisites link to `../../foundry-classic/agents/concepts/model-region-support.md` - `setup/environment-setup.mdx`, `setup/quota.mdx` - `security/configure-private-link.mdx`, `security/managed-virtual-network.mdx` - `operate/model-retirements.mdx`, `operate/screen-reader.mdx` - And many more across the `security/`, `operate/`, `guardrails/` sections
Sample of broken foundry-classic links (first 20) ``` agents/development/overview.mdx:65: ../../foundry-classic/agents/concepts/model-region-support.md agents/development/overview.mdx:71: ../../foundry-classic/agents/quotas-limits.md agents/development/overview.mdx:72: ../../foundry-classic/agents/concepts/model-region-support.md agents/development/overview.mdx:147: ../../foundry-classic/openai/how-to/content-filters.md agents/development/overview.mdx:149: ../../foundry-classic/how-to/develop/trace-agents-sdk.md get-started/developer-journey-idea-to-prototype.mdx:32: ../../foundry-classic/concepts/foundry-models-overview.md get-started/developer-journey-idea-to-prototype.mdx:50: ../../foundry-classic/index.yml operate/model-retirements.mdx:74: ../../../foundry-classic/openai/concepts/model-versions.md security/data-privacy.mdx:11: ../../../foundry-classic/concepts/foundry-models-overview.md security/configure-private-link.mdx:6: ../../foundry-classic/how-to/hub-configure-private-link.md security/managed-virtual-network.mdx:79: ../../foundry-classic/how-to/access-on-premises-resources.md setup/environment-setup.mdx:86: ../../foundry-classic/agents/concepts/model-region-support.md setup/quota.mdx:65: ../../foundry-classic/openai/how-to/quota.md ````
#### 6. Additional broken "Next Step" cards pointing to non-existent paths Several other `(Card)` "Next step" elements use invalid relative paths with `.md` extensions pointing to directories that don't exist: | File | Broken href | |------|-------------| | `agents/development/quickstart-hosted-agent.mdx` | `../how-to/manage-hosted-agent.md` | | `agents/development/publish-agent.mdx` | `../../control-plane/how-to-manage-agents.md` | | `agents/development/publish-agent.mdx` | `../../how-to/develop/install-cli-sdk.md` | | `agents/development/publish-agent.mdx` | `../../quickstarts/get-started-code.md` | | `agents/tools/fabric.mdx` | `../../concepts/tool-best-practice.md` | | `agents/tools/fabric.mdx` | `../../concepts/agent-identity.md` | | `agents/tools/web-search.mdx` | `../../concepts/tool-best-practice.md` | | `agents/tools/web-search.mdx` | `../../../agents/environment-setup.md` | | `guardrails/quickstart-blocklist.mdx` | `./how-to/use-blocklist.md` | --- ### 🟡 Confusing Areas #### 1. Two-portal confusion right on the homepage The very first page (`what-is-foundry`) introduces "Foundry (new)" and "Foundry (classic)" without a strong enough signal for a beginner about which to use. The table says "Foundry (new) is the default," but a new user who signs in and accidentally ends up in classic mode will be lost. **Recommendation**: Add a prominent callout at the top of the homepage: _"Starting fresh? Use Foundry (new). This documentation covers the new experience."_ #### 2. Three-name naming history is confusing The platform was renamed three times: Azure AI Studio → Azure AI Foundry → Microsoft Foundry. This context appears as an `(Info)` box, but web search results, tutorials, and Stack Overflow answers will use all three names. A noob who searches "Azure AI Studio quickstart" will find outdated docs. **Recommendation**: Add an explicit section in the overview explaining how to identify current vs. outdated documentation. #### 3. SDK version tables are overwhelming `api-sdk/sdk-overview` has four separate version tables (one per language), each listing 1.x GA vs. 2.x preview. For a beginner, the immediate question is: _"Which one do I install?"_ The answer is buried in a `(Note)` block rather than being the opening statement. **Recommendation**: Lead with a single, clear statement: _"If you're using the new Foundry portal, install the 2.x preview SDK."_ Then show the tables. #### 4. "MCP server" used without definition for beginners In the agent development overview and related pages, "MCP server" appears as a known term. As a newcomer, I had no idea what MCP stands for (Model Context Protocol) — and there was no inline definition or link. **Recommendation**: First use of "MCP" should expand the acronym and link to the glossary or an explainer page. #### 5. Prerequisites assume Azure admin knowledge The quickstart prerequisites reference roles like "Azure Account AI Owner," "Azure AI Owner," and Microsoft Entra concepts. For a developer who is new to Azure (and not just new to Foundry), these concepts are a wall. **Recommendation**: Add a brief callout: _"If you're on a team with an Azure administrator, ask them to complete the prerequisites and grant you the Azure AI User role."_ #### 6. Agent overview prerequisites link to unavailable content In `agents/development/overview.mdx`, the prerequisites section links to model-region-support and quotas-limits pages via `../../foundry-classic/...` paths. Since these are broken, a new user trying to check model availability before starting gets a dead end. --- ### 🟢 What Worked Well 1. **Quickstart structure** (`quickstart-create-foundry-resources.mdx`) — Excellent use of tabs (Azure CLI / Foundry portal) with numbered steps, specific command examples, and verification steps. First-time users can follow along without guessing. 2. **Working code examples in agent overview** — The Python and TypeScript examples at the top of `agents/development/overview.mdx` are copy-paste ready with clear `YOUR_PROJECT_ENDPOINT` / `YOUR_MODEL_DEPLOYMENT_NAME` placeholders. 3. **SDK comparison table** in `api-sdk/sdk-overview` — The table mapping SDK → use case → endpoint is genuinely useful for understanding the ecosystem at a glance. 4. **Prerequisites checklist with verification commands** in `api-sdk/sdk-overview` — Showing `python --version`, `node --version`, etc. as checklist items is great for beginners. 5. **Glossary** — `glossary.mdx` is comprehensive, well-organized alphabetically, and covers agent-specific terminology clearly. 6. **`overview/classic-vs-new.mdx`** — The terminology mapping table is excellent for developers coming from Azure OpenAI who need to understand what changed. --- ### Recommendations (Prioritized) | Priority | Action | |----------|--------| | 🔴 P0 | Fix broken "Next Step" cards in quickstart and get-started-code (happy path is broken) | | 🔴 P0 | Fix 299 `../../foundry-classic/...` relative links (mass 404s throughout) | | 🔴 P0 | Fix Windows backslash paths in `agents/development/overview.mdx` | | 🔴 P0 | Fill the empty `href=""` on the "Switch to classic documentation" link in `what-is-foundry.mdx` | | 🟡 P1 | Add a clear "Use Foundry (new)" callout on the homepage for brand new users | | 🟡 P1 | Rewrite SDK-overview opening to lead with the "which version to install" answer | | 🟡 P2 | Expand MCP acronym on first use in agent development docs | | 🟡 P2 | Add an Azure-admin guidance note in quickstart prerequisites | --- _Workflow run: [§22720542790](https://github.com/nicholasdbrady/foundry-docs/actions/runs/22720542790) · Analysis date: 2026-03-05 · Target: `docs-vnext/`_ > Generated by [Documentation Noob Tester](https://github.com/nicholasdbrady/foundry-docs/actions/runs/22720542790) · [◷](https://github.com/search?q=repo%3Anicholasdbrady%2Ffoundry-docs+is%3Aissue+%22gh-aw-workflow-call-id%3A+nicholasdbrady%2Ffoundry-docs%2Fdocs-noob-tester%22&type=issues) > - [x] expires on Mar 19, 2026, 1:46 PM UTC
